keyRules: Maximum 120 m above ground level (EASA Open Category)
REGISTRATION IS REQUIRED as a UAS operator if: the drone weighs ≥250 g OR the drone weighs <250 g and is equipped with a camera. Free of charge, valid for 3 years, recognized throughout the EU/EASA
Mandatory A1/A3 online training — general minimum age of 16 (reduced to 12–14 depending on drone class and subcategory, RD 517/2024).
Flights in urban areas or over densely populated areas: prior notification to the Ministry of the Interior at least 5 days in advance.
Always keep the drone within direct line of sight (VLOS)
Never fly over people, crowds, or public events
Observe the UAS no-fly zones around airports and airfields (RD 517/2024) — always check the ENAIRE Drones map.
Night flights are permitted in the open category—the drone must be equipped with a visible flashing green light.
National parks require express permission from park authorities
Canary Islands and Balearic Islands: Additional local restrictions may apply
importantNotes: Civil liability insurance is MANDATORY for subcategory A2 and for the specific and certified categories. It is not required for A1 and A3 with drones weighing less than 20 kg — but it is highly recommended.
Domestic flights: Prior notification (at least 5 days in advance) must be submitted to the Ministry of the Interior—not to AESA.
Registration with AESA is free and valid in all EU/EASA countries
Fines range from €60 to €225,000 depending on the severity of the offense (Air Safety Law 21/2003).
Military bases and government buildings: absolute no-fly zones
The GDPR applies when capturing identifiable images

fields.disclaimerText: Always check AESA's official sources before flying. This app is a tool, not a legal authority. Spain applies EASA regulations with national additions—check with AESA for current rules.
